origin.name: Barbados
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Registration Department (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Barbados travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Bridgetown guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Barbados is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from Registration Department (English language)
special_rules: Barbados issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit is required from Barbadian authorities for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed Barbadian funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Barbadian documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Bridgetown can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Barbados to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Barbados. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Registration Department, export permit, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
